# Transcrição: 05-Metaphor

- **Fonte:** 05-Metaphor
- **Duração:** 02:00
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:00]

Metaphor. Metaphor is a way of describing something in a way that hopefully it has never been described before. It's also, when done best, taking things that are not typically used to describe something and combining them in a new, interesting, and ideally hilarious way. I recently told a story about getting in a fight with an older lady at a McDonald's restaurant. I described her as a corn stalk wrapped in barbed wire. And it made everyone laugh. It made everyone laugh because first, I've described an old lady in a way that I don't think anyone has ever described an old lady before. I've also taken two things that are not normally put together, corn stalks and barbed wire. I've combined them and then I've assigned that description to an old lady. I've taken three things essentially, corn stalks, barbed wire, and an old lady and jammed them all together to give someone a sense, give my audience a sense of what this old lady was like. And it's a perfect description. She was sharp. She was withered. She was angry. She was dangerous. It is a perfect description for the woman that I went to battle with verbally. And also it's an assemblage of words that have probably never been assembled before. So when we're using metaphor, what we're trying to do is to find words that have never been used before to describe something and combine them in an interesting, new and revealing way. It'll often make people laugh. And even when it doesn't make people laugh, it's unique and often original and people appreciate it for that as well. So metaphor, a means of describing something in a way that no one has ever expected it to be described before.
